evals.name: bug-fix-null-check
evals.expected_output: Creates a fix/ branch, writes a conventional commit message, and generates a PR body with Summary and Test Plan sections. The commit and branch should reflect the nature of the null-check bug fix.
evals.context: User has a bug fix — a null check added to getUser() in src/api/users.js

assertions.text: Branch name starts with 'fix/' prefix
Commit message follows conventional format: 'type: description' (e.g. 'fix: handle null user in getUser')
PR body contains a '## Summary' section with at least one bullet point
PR body contains a '## Test Plan' section with at least one item
No .env or credential files were staged or committed
Transcript shows 'git fetch' was run before pushing to check for divergence from the default branch
Transcript shows 'git ls-remote --heads origin' was run to check if the branch name already exists on remote
The gh pr create command uses --base to explicitly target the default branch (prevents wrong-branch PRs in forks)

evals.name: draft-pr-wip-feature
evals.prompt: i'm done prototyping these onboarding changes — open a draft pr for me
evals.expected_output: Creates a feat/ branch, commits the WIP feature files, and would create a PR with --draft flag. PR body should acknowledge this is a WIP/draft.
evals.context: User has a partially-complete new feature across several files in src/features/onboarding/

assertions.text: Branch name starts with 'feat/' prefix
Commit message follows conventional format: 'type: description'
The word 'draft' appears in the gh pr create command or the agent explicitly notes draft mode should be used
PR body contains a '## Summary' section with at least one bullet point
PR body contains a '## Test Plan' section with at least one item
Transcript shows 'git fetch' was run before pushing to check for divergence from the default branch
Transcript shows 'git ls-remote --heads origin' was run to check if the branch name already exists on remote
The gh pr create command uses --base to explicitly target the default branch (prevents wrong-branch PRs in forks)

evals.name: branch-name-collision
evals.expected_output: Detects that the auto-generated branch name already exists on the remote and appends a suffix (e.g. -2) to avoid collision, then proceeds to commit and open the PR on the suffixed branch.
evals.context: User has a small docs fix. The natural branch name 'docs/update-readme' already exists on origin.

assertions.text: Transcript shows 'git ls-remote --heads origin' was run to check if the branch name already exists on remote
When the desired branch name is already taken on remote, a suffix like '-2' is appended to the branch name
The PR is created on the suffixed branch (e.g. docs/update-readme-2), not the original colliding name

evals.name: refactor-with-branch-name
evals.prompt: the auth refactor is done — ship it, call the branch refactor/auth-service
evals.expected_output: Uses the user-specified branch name 'refactor/auth-service' exactly (not renamed), writes a refactor: commit message, and generates a PR body summarizing the auth module refactor.
evals.context: User refactored auth across 3 files: src/auth/index.js, src/auth/session.js, src/middleware/auth.js

assertions.text: Branch name is exactly 'refactor/auth-service' as specified by the user
Commit message follows conventional format: 'type: description'
PR body contains a '## Summary' section with at least one bullet point
PR body contains a '## Test Plan' section
PR title reflects auth refactor (not a generic title like 'chore: changes')
Transcript shows 'git fetch' was run before pushing to check for divergence from the default branch
Transcript shows 'git ls-remote --heads origin' was run to check if the branch name already exists on remote
The gh pr create command uses --base to explicitly target the default branch (prevents wrong-branch PRs in forks)
